source_jobs.title: Channel Partnerships
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for our first Channel Partnerships hire to own and stand up Juicebox’s Partnerships function focused on HCM ecosystems and recruiting agencies. You’ll build the foundation for how Juicebox partners externally - creating co-sell motions, enablement materials, relationship strategies, and long-term channel revenue programs that help accelerate our next phase of growth.

This is a highly entrepreneurial role that blends strategy, relationship building, and execution. One day you may be building partner enablement collateral and defining partnership playbooks; the next, attending industry events, meeting with recruiting leaders, or shaping co-sell strategy alongside Sales and Marketing.

We’re looking for builders who are energized by creating structure where none exists today and motivated by the opportunity to drive meaningful ARR impact inside one of the fastest growing AI companies.




YOU WILL

 - Build and own Juicebox’s HCM and recruiting agency partnerships strategy from the ground up

 - Develop relationships with recruiting firms, talent partners, HCM consultancies, and adjacent ecosystem players

 - Create and operationalize co-sell motions that contribute meaningful pipeline and revenue growth

 - Build partner enablement materials including product education, messaging, onboarding documentation, and sales collateral

 - Partner cross-functionally with Sales, Marketing, Customer Success, and Product to ensure partner success and alignment

 - Represent Juicebox at industry events, conferences, and partner meetings

 - Identify creative ways to expand channel influence and unlock new distribution opportunities

 - Help define the systems, processes, and playbooks that will scale the partnerships organization over time




YOU ARE

 - Someone with 5–8+ years of experience in partnerships, channel sales, or strategic alliances within SaaS or technology environments

 - Excited by 0 → 1 building and comfortable creating structure in ambiguous environments

 - A strong relationship builder who knows how to develop trust and long-term strategic partnerships

 - Commercially minded with an understanding of how partnerships drive pipeline, revenue, and company growth

 - Highly organized and operationally strong - able to build documentation, processes, and repeatable motions

 - Comfortable operating cross-functionally and influencing across teams

 - Low ego, we have too much to do with too little time

 - Willing to travel periodically for partner meetings and industry events




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience working with HCM ecosystems, recruiting agencies, staffing firms, or HR technology companies

If you don’t quite hit the qualifications listed above, but feel you belong here- apply, and be prepared to share why!

source_jobs.title: Product Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Juicebox

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.



The Role

We’re looking for a Product Manager to build and own the product function at Juicebox from the ground up.

This role will be the connective tissue between customers, engineering, design, sales, and customer success teams. You’ll be the face of the product externally, the voice of the customer internally, and a key partner to our engineering and design teams as we prioritize, ship, and iterate.

This is a highly impactful, high-ownership role with real influence over our product roadmap, workflows, and long-term product strategy.



You Will

 - Translate customer business needs into clear, comprehensive product requirements (PRDs, specs, tickets)

 - Translate Juicebox’s vision into real product features

 - Partner closely with engineering and design to lead projects end-to-end

 - Act as the primary point of contact for product feedback and requests, both internally and externally

 - Spend significant time talking directly with customers to understand pain points, workflows, and unmet needs

 - Triage and prioritize bugs vs. features, balancing short-term customer needs with long-term product vision

 - Advocate for priorities backed by data, customer insight, and strong judgment

 - Work cross-functionally with sales and customer success to ensure tight feedback loops

 - Help define and guide quarterly product roadmaps

 - Establish lightweight but effective product workflows and processes as the company scales
   
   

You Have

 - 2+ years of Product Management experience (4-5+ years preferred)

 - Experience working in early-stage or high growth startup environments

 - Strong prioritization, judgment, and communication skills

 - Comfort operating in ambiguity and owning outcomes end-to-end

 - Proven ability to translate customer needs into clear product requirements

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.



The Role

Customer Success at Juicebox sits at the intersection of commercial ownership, AI workflow enablement, and customer strategy. CSMs own the full customer relationship—from onboarding and adoption to expansion and renewal—while serving as trusted advisors and AI workflow experts.

Our customers range from enterprise talent teams to startup founders, staffing firms, and VC talent partners. As a PLG business, customers start small and scale quickly, so you'll often be managing onboarding, expansion, and renewal motions simultaneously.

You'll help define what Customer Success looks like in an AI-first world at a hypergrowth company shaping a new category. Our customers love the product, embrace innovation, and genuinely enjoy partnering with us. You'll do it alongside a low-ego, collaborative team and work directly with a VP of CS who has built world-class organizations at Gong, Slack, and Zendesk.



You Will

 - Own a portfolio of customers and drive measurable outcomes across onboarding, adoption, expansion, and renewal.

 - Lead customer success plans, align on key metrics, and ensure talent leaders realize value quickly.

 - Drive adoption of AI-native workflows, guide change management, and help customers reach their first “aha” moment and beyond.

 - Own commercial conversations, including renewals, pricing negotiations, and complex buying processes.

 - Identify and close expansion opportunities across teams, stakeholders, and product offerings.

 - Build trusted, multi-threaded relationships with end users, champions, TA Ops leaders, and hiring managers.

 - Lead ROI discussions and QBRs that demonstrate impact and strengthen partnerships.

 - Help define and improve the Customer Success playbook by identifying gaps, solving problems, and scaling best practices.
   
   

You Have

 - 3+ years in Customer Success, Account Management, or Sales with ownership of a revenue target and a portfolio of 75-150 accounts.

 - A track record of exceeding commercial goals and driving customer growth.

 - The ability to manage multiple customer conversations and priorities simultaneously.

 - A builder mindset and comfort operating in ambiguity before a playbook exists.

 - Executive presence and confidence engaging senior stakeholders.

 - Experience in SaaS, consulting, HR tech, or talent tech; recruiting industry experience is a plus, not a requirement.
   
   

Location: In or willing to relocate to SF (in-person 5 days a week)

Compensation: Base + Commission for OTE targets of 165 - 230K USD. Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision. Meal + wellness stipends.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring an Enterprise Account Executive to join our growing sales team and scale Juicebox amongst our largest customers.

You’ll own mid-market and enterprise deals from our rapidly growing inbound pipeline — closing $50K–$150K+ ACV contracts.

This isn’t a traditional SaaS sales role. You'll:

 - Collaborate with Product, Marketing, and RevOps to refine our pitch and pricing

 - Have direct access to founders and engineers to unblock deals or influence roadmap

 - Get in early on a company that’s 10x'd revenue in the last 12 months and has more demand than we can handle




WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT

 - All inbound, all warm: 1,000s of leads/month, 200+ demo requests/month

 - Enterprise-ready: multiple closed 6-figure deals, with many in the pipeline

 - Massive upsell potential: 5,000+ active customers, many with much larger teams

 - Strategic buyers: You’ll sell to Heads of TA, VP People, and agency owners




YOU'RE A FIT IF YOU...

 - Have closed $50K–$300K+ ACVs and love winning competitive deals

 - Thrive in a fast-moving, founder-led GTM environment

 - Are motivated by impact and upside — not layers of sales ops

 - Have 5+ years of SaaS closing experience in B2B software

 - Have a track record of quota-crushing performance (President’s Club, Top Rep, etc.)

 - Are located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco (in-office 5 days a week required)




NICE TO HAVE

 - Have sold to the Head of TA / VP People (or HR more broadly)

 - Familiar with Product-Led-Growth (using free sign-ups as your primary channel)

 - Experience working in a small GTM team (<5 people)




COMPENSATION

 - $150-$180K base salary, based on experience.

 - Uncapped commission.

 - Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision.

 - Lunch stipend.

source_jobs.title: Head of Legal
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring our first Head of Legal to own and scale the legal function at Juicebox.

As a company operating in a rapidly evolving data and privacy landscape, legal is deeply intertwined with how we build our product, work with customers, and grow the business. This role will own legal strategy and execution across privacy and compliance, commercial contracts, vendor negotiations, and financings.

You’ll work closely with the CEO, CTO, product team, and external counsel to help navigate complex legal decisions while building scalable processes that support the next stage of company growth.

Your ultimate goal will be to ensure Juicebox can move quickly, confidently, and compliantly as we scale.




YOU WILL

 - Develop a deep understanding of the data privacy and regulatory landscape impacting Juicebox

 - Help make product feature and capability decisions while ensuring regulatory compliance

 - Own all legal workflows across the company

 - Partner with outside counsel on privacy, regulatory, employment, corporate, and financing matters (we currently work with 4 external law firms)

 - Negotiate and manage data vendor agreements that are critical to our product and business

 - Own commercial contract negotiations with customers and support the sales team in driving deals to close

 - Lead the legal components of venture financings, corporate governance, and strategic transactions

 - Build scalable legal processes, systems, and operating frameworks as the company grows

 - Partner closely with product, engineering, sales, and leadership teams to evaluate risk and make practical business decisions

 - Serve as a trusted advisor to founders on legal, operational, and strategic matters




YOU HAVE

 - 6+ years of legal experience, ideally across both a top law firm and a high-growth technology company

 - Experience advising technology companies on data privacy, compliance, and commercial contracts

 - Strong understanding of the evolving regulatory landscape surrounding data and privacy

 - Experience negotiating complex commercial agreements and vendor contracts

 - Familiarity with venture financings, corporate governance, and general corporate matters

 - Strong business judgment and the ability to balance legal risk with company growth objectives

 - A pragmatic, solutions-oriented approach to problem solving

 - High ownership mentality and comfort operating as a team of one

 - Excitement about working cross-functionally and being deeply embedded in the day-to-day operations of the business




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience with start-ups or high growth companies

source_jobs.title: GTM Recruiter
source_jobs.description:
About Juicebox

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 6,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

About the Role

We’re hiring a Go-To-Market Recruiter to help build out our GTM teams. This isn’t a traditional recruiting role. Juicebox is an AI sourcing platform used by thousands of recruiters, founders, and talent leaders, you’ll become a visible leader in the recruiting community, working at the center of where modern sourcing and AI intersect.

You’ll help shape how we show up externally, through content, community, and candidate experience, to make Juicebox the top destination for top-tier talent in AI.

You’re a Fit If You…

 - Have 2+ years of experience recruiting (either in-house or agency)

 - Have a strong personal brand in the recruiting community (e.g., active LinkedIn presence, trusted network, respected by peers)

 - Have excellent written and verbal communication - you can make a candidate feel seen and understood

 - Thrive in fast-paced, zero-to-one environments

 - Care deeply about quality and candidate experience

 - Are excited to work in-office 5 days a week with the team in SF

Nice to Have

 - Have worked with early-stage startups (Seed - Series B) or in-house at high-growth companies

 - Familiar with AI, SaaS, or B2B talent markets

 - Enjoy writing or speaking about hiring, talent, or the future of work

Compensation & Benefits

 - Medical, dental, vision

 - Lunch stipend

 - Office in downtown San Francisco (5 mins from Montgomery BART)

 - Annual offsite + team events

source_jobs.title: Engineering Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Juicebox

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

About the role

We're looking to add a strong senior IC who can take over major parts of the codebase and help maintain technical velocity as we scale. This person will work closely with the founders, act as a high-trust reviewer, and raise the bar for engineering quality and speed. You’ll shape architectural decisions and mentor teammates — without needing to manage people. Perfect for someone who wants autonomy, impact, and to be part of a tight, flat org shipping fast.



What we’re looking for (technical)

 - You have hands-on experience building features / leading projects from scratch

 - You have a deep understanding of systems and infrastructure

 - You have experience with JS frameworks and full-stack web apps

 - You’ve been in fast-paced startup environments



What we’re looking for (non technical)

 - You’re good at (1) learning things quickly and (2) getting things done.

 - You've shown excellence: be it in your education, work experiences, projects, or anything else you can share.

 - You’re good at communication and enjoy working in person.



Nice to haves

 - You were / want to be a founder.

 - You have experience with large-scale data pipelines or search clusters.



Compensation & Benefits

 - Medical, dental, vision

 - Lunch stipend

 - Office in downtown San Francisco

source_jobs.title: Founding Brand Designer
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Founding Brand Designer to define what Juicebox stands for and ensure that shows up everywhere our company exists in the world.

You will be responsible for building the foundation of the Juicebox brand: what we believe, how we communicate, and how we visually express ourselves across every touchpoint. Juicebox is a premium brand: reflected in our product, how we show up for customers, and every material we publish.

This is a foundational role with high visibility and ownership. In this role, you’ll partner closely with product, marketing, sales, leadership, and external vendors to bring the brand to life across every touchpoint - from campaigns to content to product storytelling. You’ll shape how the market understands Juicebox—and how we understand ourselves.




YOU WILL

Define the Brand

 - Audit and deeply understand the current Juicebox brand: how it shows up internally and externally

 - Define what Juicebox stands for: our positioning, narrative, and brand principles

 - Establish a clear, distinct brand voice, visuals, and tone that scales across channels

Bring the Brand to Life

 - Partner with content marketing to shape high-quality content (video, thought leadership, campaigns)

 - Work with GTM and sales teams to ensure brand shows up clearly in how we sell and communicate

 - Translate brand strategy into execution across marketing channels and influencer / partnership campaigns

 - Align stakeholders around a unified brand direction and ensure adoption across teams




YOU HAVE

 - 3+ years of experience in brand marketing, design, creative strategy, or similar roles

 - Deep understanding of brand voice, positioning, and visual identity

 - Ability to operate both strategically and hands-on—equally comfortable defining direction and executing details

 - Strong cross-functional communication and stakeholder management skills

 - Experience working in fast-paced, high-growth environments

source_jobs.title: GTM Strategy
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

This role sits at the intersection of Sales Enablement, Revenue Operations, Product Marketing, and AI Strategy. You’ll partner closely with GTM leadership to rethink how our sales organization operates, scales, and leverages AI to drive the next phase of Juicebox’s growth.

You’ll completely revamp the enablement experience, from onboarding programs to sales playbooks to messaging infrastructure, ensuring our GTM teams are equipped to execute at a world-class level.

Beyond enablement, this role will help shape the future of AI-powered GTM execution at Juicebox. You’ll work alongside senior leadership to identify operational bottlenecks, architect agentic workflows, and build systems that help the business scale faster, sell larger deals, and operate more intelligently.

This is a highly strategic and execution-oriented role: part operator, part enablement leader, part GTM architect. We’re looking for builders who love solving hard operational problems, thrive in ambiguity, and want outsized influence on the trajectory of a generational AI company.



You Will

 - Revamp and scale Juicebox’s GTM enablement program across onboarding, training, messaging, and execution

 - Build scalable onboarding experiences and sales playbooks that help new hires ramp quickly and effectively

 - Partner closely with Product Marketing and Product teams to translate messaging and positioning into actionable sales enablement materials

 - Own and continuously improve core sales collateral including central pitch decks, talk tracks, and enablement documentation

 - Help define Juicebox’s AI strategy for GTM, identifying opportunities to leverage automation, agentic workflows, and operational intelligence across the revenue organization

 - Partner with GTM leadership to identify key business bottlenecks and architect systems that unlock faster execution and larger revenue outcomes

 - Design workflows and infrastructure that improve seller productivity, meeting preparation, CRM hygiene, pipeline visibility, and operational efficiency

 - Work cross-functionally with Sales, RevOps, Marketing, Product, and Leadership to operationalize scalable GTM processes




YOU HAVE

 - 7+ years of experience across Sales Enablement, Revenue Operations, Product Marketing, GTM Strategy, or related functions within high-growth SaaS environments

 - Deep experience in enablement and excited to build scalable onboarding and training systems from the ground up

 - Strong strategic and systems-orientation, you naturally think about workflows, infrastructure, and operational leverage

 - Passion about AI-native ways of working and energized by the opportunity to rethink how GTM teams operate

 - Comfort navigating ambiguity and solving undefined operational problems




NICE TO HAVE

 - Background spanning multiple GTM disciplines such as enablement, RevOps, PMM, or sales strategy

 - Experience implementing AI workflows, automation systems, or operational tooling within revenue organizations

If you don’t quite hit the qualifications listed above, but feel you belong here- apply, and be prepared to share why!



Lo

source_jobs.title: Account Executive
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT THE ROLE

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.



GTM at Juicebox

 - All pipeline is inbound through either our free trial or our demo form; 200+ inbound demos each month.

 - Core customer groups include in-house TA teams and recruiting agencies.

 - Deal cycles are 2-3 weeks (depending on whether the prospect does a trial).

 - There's a large opportunity to upsell our existing 2,000+ customers, many of which have larger recruiting teams.

 - We consistently win against legacy recruiting tech products.
   
   

You Have

 - Experience closing ACVs of $10K - $50K

 - 2+ years experience in SaaS sales

 - Track record of exceeding quota (Presidents Club, Top 10%, MVP, etc.)

 - Located in or willing to relocate to SF
   
   

Nice to Haves

 - Have sold to the Head of TA / VP People (or HR more broadly)

 - Familiar with Product-Led-Growth (using free sign-ups as your primary channel)

 - Experience working in a small GTM team (<5 people)
   
   

Compensation: $90-$125K base salary, based on experience. Uncapped commission. Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision. Lunch stipend.

source_jobs.title: Sales Development Representative
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Sales Development Representative to join our growing sales team and fuel Juicebox’s next stage of growth.

You’ll own top-of-funnel pipeline generation - crafting strategic outbound outreach to Heads of Talent, VP People, and recruiting agency owners.

This isn’t a cookie-cutter SDR role. You’ll:

 - Partner closely with AEs, Product, and Marketing to engage prospects

 - Help refine our outbound playbook alongside our AEs

 - Get in early on a company that’s 10x’d revenue in the last 12 months and has more demand than we can handle

We’re seeing strong pull from both in-house TA teams and recruiting agencies — and many of our thousands of customers are ready for expansion.

If you're looking for a high-leverage role at a product-first company with real traction, this is it.




WHY THIS ROLE IS DIFFERENT

 - Career upside: Clear paths into AE or SDR Management roles within 12 months

 - Strategic buyers: You’ll connect with Heads of TA, VP People, and agency owners

 - Shape the playbook: Help build our outbound strategy from scratch




YOU’RE A FIT IF YOU…

 - Have 1+ years of sales or customer-facing experience (SaaS or startup is a plus)

 - Are a strong communicator: clear, concise, and persuasive

 - Thrive in a fast-paced, founder-led GTM environment

 - Love testing new strategies and learning from the data

 - Have experience with CRMs (HubSpot, Salesforce) and prospecting tools (Apollo, Clay)

 - Are located in or willing to relocate to San Francisco (in-office 5 days a week required)




NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience selling to TA / HR personas

 - Experience at an early-stage startup or GTM team <5

 - Comfortable navigating Product-Led Growth environments (e.g., free sign-ups, trials)




COMPENSATION & BENEFITS

 - $70K–$90K base salary, based on experience

 - $110 - $130K OTE

 - Uncapped commission, driven through qualified meetings booked quota

 - Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision

 - Lunch stipend

source_jobs.title: Content Marketing Manager
source_jobs.description:
About Juicebox

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.



The Role

We’re hiring a Content Marketing Manager to become the voice of Juicebox.

This role owns the content that prospects and customers experience outside the product, including blog posts, case studies, videos, SEO content, and more.

You’ll work closely with the marketing team and our customers to tell the stories of how companies are using Juicebox to transform their hiring.



You Will

 - Own Juicebox’s content strategy and execution

 - Write and publish blog https://juicebox.ai/blog/articles posts, case studies, and SEO content

 - Interview customers and create compelling customer stories https://juicebox.ai/customers

 - Produce additional content formats including videos, guides, and thought leadership

 - Manage and edit work from external writers and contributors, including social media content

 - Identify opportunities for new content initiatives and distribution channels

 - Ensure all published content reflects Juicebox’s voice, brand, and product expertise

 - Help make SEO a major customer acquisition channel
   

You Have

 - 2+ years of B2B content marketing experience (4+ preferred)

 - Strong writing and storytelling ability

 - Experience producing SEO-driven content

 - Familiarity with tools like Semrush, Profound, or similar SEO / AEO platforms

 - Ability to work with external writers and maintain a consistent voice

 - Strong ownership mentality and ability to operate independently

source_jobs.title: Senior Software Engineer
source_jobs.description:
About Juicebox

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.



The Role: Senior Software Engineer

As a Senior SWE you’ll play a key role in engineering decisions, working closely with the founders and focus on questions like:

 - How can we optimize the ranking of our profile results?

 - What new filters or data sources can we add to our search engine?

 - How can we delight our users in the first 5 minutes of signing up to Juicebox?

You will design, build, and ship product features from end to end — many of which will be built on LLMs.

Recent projects have included fine-tuning a model to extract relevant insights from profiles and building feedback loops for improving search queries.

We work with Next.js + React, Node.js, OpenSearch (Elasticsearch), AWS SQS, Spark, and a number of inference providers + LLMs, etc.



About You

 - You've built something from scratch — a startup, project, or feature.

 - You enjoy working hard and owning projects.

 - You are hands-on, move fast, and learn best by building.

 - You've shown excellence: be it in your education, work experiences, projects, or anything else you can share.

 - You take asymmetric bets.

 - You have some experience with the core of our stack: Next.js, AWS, etc.

source_jobs.title: Sales Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

We have 5,000+ customers spanning from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies with a global presence. Teams at Ramp, Notion, Samsara, leading AI labs, and more trust Juicebox to help them find, assess, and engage with talent so they can build world-class organizations.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding with lead investments from Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




THE ROLE

A career-defining opportunity to be a founding member of the Sales Leadership team, reporting directly to the VP of Sales.

This role will be responsible for overseeing and developing our core Account Executive team to drive new logo growth. You’ll partner closely with GTM cross functional peers, as well as the founders to help scale Juicebox into its next chapter.

This is a multi-altitudinal role, tactical and strategic. You’ll work directly with reps on pipeline management, deal execution, forecasting, coaching, customer meetings, and sales strategy, while also helping architect how our broader sales organization scales over time.

We’re looking for builders who are motivated to elevate the GTM craft within one of the fastest growing AI companies. You’ll shape culture, get runway to implement new ideas, and have fun doing it.




YOU WILL

 - Lead and develop a team of Account Executives in our San Francisco headquarters

 - Own your Business: Pipeline Generation, Forecasting, Deal Execution, Team performance

 - Set the Pace: Create a rhythm on your team complete with 1:1s, team meetings, deal reviews, coaching/development and more

 - Build the Business: Establish playbooks, sales motions, and systems/processes alongside key peers in Revenue Operations, Customer Success, Sales Development, Marketing and more.

 - Get Creative: We’re always looking for fresh perspectives to scale our business effectively, and fast!


YOU ARE

 - Someone with 5–7+ years of experience in SaaS sales environments, 2-3+ in leadership

 - Comfortable scaling within early-stage or high-growth startup environments

 - Low-ego- we have too much to do with too little time

 - Intellectually curious; you chase big questions come up with unique solutions

 - High performance and people oriented

 - Comfortable operating in ambiguity and fast-moving environments

 - AI fluent and enthusiasm for modern GTM tooling

 - A strong communicator who can motivate and develop high-performing teams


NICE TO HAVE

 - Experience helping scale a company from Series B through later growth stages

 - Background at companies known for strong product-led or modern GTM cultures

 - Strong opinions around sales tooling, systems, and GTM infrastructure

If you don’t quite hit the qualifications listed above, but feel you belong here- apply, and be prepared to share why!

source_jobs.title: Software Engineer, Integrations
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war. In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. We’re building the tools that help companies find, engage, and hire great people faster.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Software Engineer, Integrations to build and own critical external integrations that help customers get value from Juicebox quickly and reliably.

This role sits at the intersection of engineering, product, and real-world customer workflows. You’ll work on ATS integrations, mailbox integrations, and SSO, and help make messy external systems work in production. That includes building integration logic, debugging sync issues, handling edge cases, and developing scalable patterns around complex external dependencies.

This is a strong fit for someone who likes solving ambiguous technical problems, sweating the details, and working across systems and stakeholders.




YOU HAVE

 - Strong engineering fundamentals and comfort working in code

 - Experience with APIs, webhooks, OAuth, SSO, or third-party integrations

 - Excellent attention to detail and strong debugging instincts

 - Clear written and verbal communication

 - High ownership and persistence in ambiguous environments


BONUS POINTS IF YOU

 - Have experience with ATS or HR tech systems

 - Have worked on mailbox, identity, or integration-heavy products

 - Have experience in a startup or highly cross-functional technical role

source_jobs.title: Security Engineer
source_jobs.description:
Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game — you either compete or lose.

Teams at companies like Ramp and Notion, along with leading AI labs, use Juicebox to power their hiring. We support 5,000+ customers ranging from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We're on a path to $100M ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

We've raised $116M+ from top-tier investors including Sequoia, DST Global, NFDG, and Y Combinator.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re looking for a Security Engineer to build the security foundation for Juicebox from first principles.

You’ll work directly on the systems that secure our infrastructure, data, and products; designing secure-by-default primitives that scale with the company without slowing engineering down.

You’ll operate across identity, cloud infrastructure, application security, observability, and internal platform systems.

The ideal person has strong security instincts, a bias toward automation, and engineering experience.


YOU MAY BE A FIT IF

 - You're passionate about LLMs and securing agentic systems

 - You have strong opinions about building systems that are both secure and developer-friendly.

 - You have battle-tested infrastructure or platform engineering instincts.

 - You think like an attacker and surface security vulnerabilities before they happen

 - You have experience securing systems in fast-paced environments

 - You’re comfortable working without heavy PM overhead; you will take ownership in solving the underlying business goal


SAMPLE PROJECTS INCLUDE

 - Develop sandboxing and secure execution patterns for AI-powered workflows and agents.

 - Implement secure-by-default infrastructure guardrails across AWS using Terraform and GitOps workflows.

 - Build automated systems that reduce operational risk.

 - Create paved-road security tooling that helps engineers ship securely by default.

 - Build a unified identity and access system across internal tooling, infrastructure, and production systems.

 - Design secrets management and credential rotation systems with strong auditing and least-privilege guarantees.

 - Improve application security around authentication, authorization, API protection, and multi-tenant isolation.

 - Build security observability pipelines for logging, detection, and incident response.


WHAT WE’RE LOOKING FOR

 - Strong background in application security, infrastructure, DevOps, or platform engineering.

 - Experience designing and shipping production systems end-to-end.

 - Hands-on experience with AWS infrastructure and security primitives (IAM, networking, RDS, etc.).

 - Experience with infrastructure-as-code tools like Terraform.

 - Familiarity with identity systems such as SSO, IAM, or IdPs.

 - Strong communication skills and the ability to drive projects independently.


BONUS POINTS FOR

 - Interest in AI/LLM security, prompt injection, data poisoning, model extraction, etc. and mitigations.

 - Experience with tools like Wiz, Doppler, or 1Password.

 - Experience with SIEM or security event pipelines.

 - Kubernetes or service mesh security experience.

 - Experience with sandboxing or secure execution environments.


WHY THIS ROLE

 - Build foundational security systems at one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies worldwide.

 - Shape systems that will support the next decade of company growth.

 - Work on hard technical problems with real ownership and impact.

 - Join a small team that values speed, autonomy, and strong engineering taste.

source_jobs.title: Growth Product Lead
source_jobs.description:
About Juicebox

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

The Role

We’re hiring a Growth Product Lead to own and scale Juicebox’s organic and product-led growth engine.

A significant portion of Juicebox revenue already comes from our self-serve product motion. This role will focus on growing that channel through product experiments, lifecycle marketing, and user-driven growth loops.

You’ll work closely with the founders and marketing team to design experiments, launch initiatives, and drive measurable growth.

Your ultimate goal will be to increase revenue from self-serve customers, and you will own that metric.

You Will

 - Own Juicebox’s self-serve revenue growth

 - Drive organic and product-led growth initiatives

 - Increase user invitations and product-driven adoption

 - Improve onboarding, activation, and referral flows

 - Run experiments to improve self-serve acquisition, retention, and reactivation

 - Launch lifecycle campaigns across email, product messaging, and promotions

 - Analyze product usage and growth metrics to identify opportunities

 - Work cross-functionally with marketing, product, and engineering to ship growth experiments

You Have

 - 2+ years working on growth at a B2B or B2C SaaS company (4+ preferred)

 - Experience with product-led growth or growth experimentation

 - Comfort working with product and user data

 - Ability to read or write SQL queries (or interpret them)

 - Strong ownership mentality and ability to run experiments end-to-end

 - High EQ and ability to collaborate across teams to ship initiatives

source_jobs.title: Manager, Customer Success
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose. Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies. We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world. Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

The Role

This is a front-line manager role for a CS team that owns the full revenue motion: expansions and renewals across a book of mid-market and enterprise customers. You'll manage a team of CSMs who are part consultant, part account manager, part AI workflow expert, and you'll be the person who makes them great. Expect to be in deals, on calls, and in the weeds with your team when it matters. You'll also be a builder. We're writing the CS playbook as we fly the plane and we need a manager who has a point of view on what great looks like and the drive to make it real. You report directly to the VP of CS.

You'll define what customer success looks like in an AI-first world at a hypergrowth company becoming a category leader. The foundation is already in place: customers love the product, relationships are strong, and commercial results speak for themselves. Your opportunity is to scale that into a world-class CS organization alongside a low-ego, collaborative team, working directly with a VP of Customer Success who previously built leading CS organizations at Gong, Slack, and Zendesk.

You Will:

 - Coach and develop your team: Run deal reviews, listen to calls, and deliver direct, actionable feedback that elevates performance.

 - Own team performance: Drive GRR, NRR, and expansion goals through accurate forecasting and strong pipeline management.

 - Be a player-coach: Jump into complex renewals and expansions, helping CSMs navigate stakeholders, pricing, and negotiations.

 - Build the playbook: Continuously improve renewal, expansion, QBR, success planning, and forecasting processes.

 - Partner cross-functionally: Collaborate with Sales, Product, and leadership to improve the customer experience and shape GTM strategy.

You Have:

 - 5+ years in Customer Success, Account Management, or Sales, including 2+ years managing a revenue-owning team. We care more about what you've built than your title.

 - Proven success leading teams that consistently exceed GRR, NRR, and expansion targets.

 - Strong commercial instincts with a track record of coaching others to improve.

 - A builder who has introduced processes and programs that measurably improved a CS organization.

 - High standards, high accountability, and a coaching style that elevates team performance.

 - Executive presence with customers, cross-functional partners, and senior leadership.

Location: In or willing to relocate to SF (in-person 5 days a week)

Compensation: Base + Commission for 215 - 315K OTE target, depending on experience. Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision. Lunch stipend.

source_jobs.title: Business Operations Lead, UK
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We're hiring a UK Business Operations Lead to own our London office launch and build the foundation for our business in EMEA.

This is a true generalist role. You'll operate as an extension of the CEO across GTM, operations, and product, owning whatever ambiguous, high-stakes work is in front of you. As our first non-sales hire on the ground in the UK, you'll have outsized ownership, directly influencing how we scale internationally, starting with where we go next after London.



You Will

 - Partner with the CEO and leadership team to inform UK strategy, set priorities, and drive key decisions about how we show up in this market

 - Own our London launch strategy, including launch events, out-of-home campaigns, and paid ads investment

 - Identify and resolve privacy and compliance changes needed to operate in the UK

 - Unblock the sales team and fix root causes in partnership with the US team

 - Be the primary point of contact for legal, finance, and other US-based functions on everything happening in the UK

 - Evaluate and recommend the next market for European expansion, and build the plan to get there

 - Advocate for the product features the UK and EMEA need to win

 - Stand up whatever functions, workflows, and systems London needs as the office grows



You Have

 - 5+ years in a generalist, BizOps, or founder's-office role at a fast-growing company (experience launching a new market or office a strong plus)

 - A track record of taking ambiguous, cross-functional problems from zero to done

 - Comfort moving between big, visible bets (like a flagship launch campaign) and unglamorous operational fixes

 - Experience working cross-functionally with a US-based team across time zones

 - A structured approach to evaluating new markets and building plans from scratch

 - Founder energy: you're comfortable being the first person on the ground and building the playbook as you go

 - Strong communication skills and the confidence to push a US-based team on what this market needs

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Programs Lead (Scale)
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose. Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies. We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world. Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

The Role

We're building the systems that turn great individual CSM work into a repeatable, scalable customer success motion. You'll own the customer journey, health scoring, lifecycle automation, and scaled programs that drive adoption, retention, and expansion across our managed customer base. Sitting at the intersection of strategy, tooling, and AI, you'll partner closely with our CSMs and CS Programs team to make every customer touchpoint smarter and more impactful.

As Juicebox's first Scaled Customer Success hire, you'll build this function from the ground up with full ownership and a direct line to the VP of Customer Success. Your work will shape NRR across our managed customers, unlock significant Agent adoption opportunities, and build the scalable foundation that grows with the business. You'll do this alongside a world-class CS Programs team focused on education, community, and champion programs, and a VP of CS who has built leading Customer Success organizations at Gong, Slack, and Zendesk.

You Will

 - Own customer health scoring: Build the framework that gives CSMs earlier risk detection and clearer expansion opportunities through account health, adoption signals, renewal risk, and activation insights.

 - Scale CSM engagement: Design the automations, sequences, and triggered workflows that help CSMs effectively manage customers from SMB through enterprise.

 - Build lifecycle automation: Create onboarding, adoption, renewal, expansion, and re-engagement programs that keep customers progressing between CSM touchpoints.

 - Unify customer intelligence: Bring together product usage, Slack, Gong, and other customer signals to surface trends, sentiment, and actionable insights in one place.

 - Drive commercial growth: Build automated renewal, expansion, upgrade, and Agent activation motions that create the right opportunities for CSMs to engage and grow ARR.

 - Partner cross-functionally: Work closely with CS Programs, Growth, Product, and Marketing to deliver a seamless, AI-powered customer journey across every touchpoint.

You Have

 - 4+ years in Digital Customer Success, Scaled CS, Lifecycle Marketing, Customer Marketing, CS Strategy & Ops, Growth, or a CS leadership role at a high-growth SaaS company.

 - Proven experience building scaled customer programs and automation from the ground up.

 - Hands-on experience with AI and CS tooling such as Claude, HubSpot, Pylon, Gainsight, or similar platforms.

 - Founder mentality with the ability to thrive in ambiguity and build systems before a playbook exists.

 - Strong perspective on what modern, AI-native Customer Success should look like.

 - Analytical and data-driven, with a track record of measuring impact and prioritizing the highest-leverage opportunities.

Location: In or willing to relocate to SF (in-person 5 days a week)

Compensation: 180-280K base salary, based on experience. Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision. Lunch stipend.

source_jobs.title: Customer Success Programs Lead (Education)
source_jobs.description:
JOB DESCRIPTION




ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose. Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies. We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world. Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

The Role

We're building the Programs layer that turns great individual Customer Success work into a repeatable, scalable motion. This role sits at the intersection of customer education, community, and advocacy: three levers that drive adoption, expansion, and retention at scale. You'll be the first person dedicated to this at Juicebox, which means you're building from scratch with full ownership and a direct line to the VP of CS and leadership team.

You're building something that doesn't exist yet at a company growing 20% month over month. The programs you build will directly accelerate NRR, reduce CSM burden, and create a customer community around a product people genuinely love.You http://love.You're building something that doesn't exist yet at a company growing 20% month over month. The programs you build will directly accelerate NRR, reduce CSM burden, and create a customer community around a product people genuinely love.

You Will

 - Customer Education: Build scalable programs that reduce CSM lift and empower customers to self-serve through onboarding academies, playbooks, in-product education, webinars, and office hours—driving faster time to value and product adoption.

 - Community Programs: Build and grow customer communities that foster peer learning, advocacy, and virality through forums, Slack groups, user events, and customer-led content.

 - Champion Programs: Identify and activate customer champions with exclusive access, recognition, and opportunities such as CABs, betas, events, case studies, and references to drive adoption and expansion.

You Have

 - 4+ years in a CS Programs, Customer Education, Community, Champions or Lifecycle role at a fast growing company.

 - Experience building at least one of these motions from scratch, not just inheriting one.

 - Executive presence and the ability to hold a room with senior stakeholders

 - Strong content and communication instincts: you know how to make complex things simple and engaging.

 - High energy with the ability to juggle many projects and conversations at once

 - Founder mentality: comfortable operating before the playbook exists. Comfortable operating independently without a lot of structure.

 - A point of view on what great community and education looks like and the portfolio to back it up.

Location: In or willing to relocate to SF (in-person 5 days a week)

Compensation: 180K - 280K base salary, based on experience. Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision. Lunch stipend.

source_jobs.title: Enterprise Customer Success Manager
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.

 

The Role

Customer Success at Juicebox sits at the intersection of commercial ownership, AI workflow enablement, and customer strategy. CSMs own the full customer relationship—from onboarding and adoption to expansion and renewal—while serving as trusted advisors and AI workflow experts.

Our customers range from enterprise talent teams to startup founders, staffing firms, and VC talent partners. As a PLG business, customers start small and scale quickly, so you'll often be managing onboarding, expansion, and renewal motions simultaneously.

You'll help define what Customer Success looks like in an AI-first world at a hypergrowth company shaping a new category. Our customers love the product, embrace innovation, and genuinely enjoy partnering with us. You'll do it alongside a low-ego, collaborative team and work directly with a VP of CS who has built world-class organizations at Gong, Slack, and Zendesk.

 

You Will

 - Own a portfolio of customers and drive measurable outcomes across onboarding, adoption, expansion, and renewal.

 - Lead customer success plans, align on key metrics, and ensure talent leaders realize value quickly.

 - Drive adoption of AI-native workflows, guide change management, and help customers reach their first “aha” moment and beyond.

 - Own commercial conversations, including renewals, pricing negotiations, and complex buying processes.

 - Identify and close expansion opportunities across teams, stakeholders, and product offerings.

 - Build trusted, multi-threaded relationships with end users, champions, TA Ops leaders, and hiring managers.

 - Lead ROI discussions and QBRs that demonstrate impact and strengthen partnerships.

 - Help define and improve the Customer Success playbook by identifying gaps, solving problems, and scaling best practices.
   
    

You Have

 - 6+ years in Customer Success, Strategic Account Management, or Enterprise SaaS with ownership of renewals, expansion revenue, and a portfolio of high-value enterprise accounts.

 - Experience managing complex stakeholder relationships, driving executive engagement, and growing revenue within strategic customers.

 - A track record of exceeding commercial goals and driving customer growth.

 - The ability to manage multiple customer conversations and priorities simultaneously.

 - A builder mindset and comfort operating in ambiguity before a playbook exists.

 - Executive presence and confidence engaging senior stakeholders.

 - Experience in SaaS, consulting, HR tech, or talent tech; recruiting industry experience is a plus, not a requirement.
   
    

Location: In or willing to relocate to SF (in-person 5 days a week)

Compensation: Base + Commission for OTE targets of 205 - 265K USD. Generous benefits, including medical, dental, and vision. Lunch stipend.

source_jobs.title: Product Marketing Lead
source_jobs.description:
ABOUT JUICEBOX

Juicebox is on a mission to help teams win the talent war.

In the age of AI, human ingenuity is the ultimate scarce resource. Recruiting is a zero-sum game where you either compete or lose.

Teams at Ramp, Notion, and leading AI labs use Juicebox to power their hiring, alongside 5,000+ customers from early-stage startups to Fortune 500 companies.

We are on the path to $100M in ARR with 20%+ monthly growth, powering thousands of searches every day and making Juicebox one of the fastest-growing AI SaaS companies in the world.

Juicebox has raised over $116M in funding from lead investors including Sequoia Capital, DST Global, and NFDG. Read more about our $80M Series B https://juicebox.ai/blog/series-b here. Additional investors in Juicebox include Y Combinator, Coatue, Bond, Lux, Verified Capital, and Committed Capital.




ABOUT THE ROLE

We’re hiring a Product Marketing Lead to own and define how Juicebox shows up in the market, across product launches, core messaging, and competitive positioning.

At its core, Juicebox is a PLG company - making word of mouth one of our most important channels. We care deeply about how our users think about and advocate for our product. For that to be successful, we need clear messaging for our user base, ranging from freelance recruiters to VPs of TA at the worlds’ largest companies.

This is a high-ownership, 0→1 role where you’ll shape the narrative for what we’re building, why it matters, and how we win. You’ll sit at the center of Product and Sales, ensuring we tell a clear, compelling story internally and externally, especially as we introduce new capabilities and define when and how customers should use them.




YOU WILL

 - Own the narrative around new and existing product features: what we’re building, why it matters, and how it’s differentiated. For example, how does our new Agent https://juicebox.ai/agent product compare to the existing search workflow?

 - Lead end-to-end product launches, from positioning and messaging to cross-functional coordination and execution. Juicebox rolls out new features weekly!

 - Build and own competitive positioning and messaging, enabling the team to clearly articulate why we win. This includes both sales-distributed content, as well as our online presence and ads.

 - Partner closely with Sales to translate product capabilities into clear, effective sales narratives and collateral

 - Identify gaps in current messaging, audit what’s working vs. not, and build a clear roadmap to test and improve it

 - Establish structure and systems for product marketing in a fast-moving, often ambiguous environment




YOU HAVE

 - 4+ years of experience in product marketing or similar role

 - Strong instincts for positioning and messaging—you can take complex ideas and make them simple and compelling

 - Experience working closely with product and sales teams, with a deep understanding of how messaging impacts revenue

 - Excellent communication skills, both written and verbal

 - Strong judgment and prioritization—you know what matters and how to focus on it

 - Experience in startups, B2B SaaS, or AI is a plus

 - Based in San Francisco or open to relocating (in-office 5 days/week)

notes: High growth AI SaaS company with strong sales focus
